title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a message on an answering machine from a dance teacher.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hi Luke, it’s Hannah from the dance studio. I know you enrolled for the beginners’ class on Wednesday, but the hall is being used for a school concert that evening. ||Woman: The class hasn’t been cancelled. [1]We’re moving it to Tuesday this week, at the usual time of half past six[/1]. Please call if that day is impossible for you; otherwise I’ll see you then.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hi Luke, it’s Hannah from the dance studio. I know you enrolled for the beginners’ class on Wednesday, but the hall is being used for a school concert that evening.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The class hasn’t been cancelled. We’re moving it to Tuesday this week, at the usual time of half past six. Please call if that day is impossible for you; otherwise I’ll see you then.

questions.question: What change does Hannah tell Luke about?

options.A: The class will take place on another day.
options.B: The class will start later than expected.

questions.explanation: Hannah says the class is moving to Tuesday this week at its usual time (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a new sports app.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Have you tried that new running app everyone’s talking about? It shows maps of local routes and tells you how far you’ve gone. ||Boy: Yes, I downloaded it last week. The route suggestions are useful, and I don’t mind the adverts. [2]It keeps comparing my times with other users, though, and that takes the enjoyment out of running for me[/2]. ||Girl: Perhaps you can switch that feature off.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Have you tried that new running app everyone’s talking about? It shows maps of local routes and tells you how far you’ve gone.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes, I downloaded it last week. The route suggestions are useful, and I don’t mind the adverts. It keeps comparing my times with other users, though, and that takes the enjoyment out of running for me.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Perhaps you can switch that feature off.

questions.question: What does the boy dislike about the app?
questions.explanation: He says comparing his times with other users removes his enjoyment (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement in a shopping centre.
extracts.audio_script: Announcer: Attention please. A set of keys with a red bicycle key ring has been found beside the information desk on the ground floor. If you think they are yours, please speak to a member of staff. ||Announcer: [3]You’ll be asked to describe one other item attached to the key ring before the keys can be returned[/3]. The information desk will remain open until the centre closes at eight.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: Attention please. A set of keys with a red bicycle key ring has been found beside the information desk on the ground floor. If you think they are yours, please speak to a member of staff.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: You’ll be asked to describe one other item attached to the key ring before the keys can be returned. The information desk will remain open until the centre closes at eight.

questions.question: What must the owner of the keys do?

options.A: Say what else is on the key ring.
options.B: Collect them before the shops close.
options.C: Tell staff exactly where they were lost.

questions.explanation: The owner must describe another item attached to the key ring (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a woman who learned to sail.
extracts.audio_script: Interviewer: So, Chloe, you recently completed a sailing course. Was it something you’d always planned to do? ||Woman: Not really. My friends wanted a holiday by the sea and suggested lessons. I didn’t expect to enjoy them, as I’m not keen on cold water. ||Woman: But [4]having to work together to control the boat was what I liked most[/4]. I’ll probably sign up for another course.

dialogue.speaker: interviewer
dialogue.text: So, Chloe, you recently completed a sailing course. Was it something you’d always planned to do?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. My friends wanted a holiday by the sea and suggested lessons. I didn’t expect to enjoy them, as I’m not keen on cold water.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: But having to work together to control the boat was what I liked most. I’ll probably sign up for another course.

questions.question: What did Chloe most enjoy about sailing?
questions.explanation: Chloe says she most liked working together to control the boat (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer speaking to an assistant in a clothes shop.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Excuse me, I bought these trousers yesterday, but when I tried them on at home, they were far too long. I like the style, though. ||Assistant: We can exchange them for a shorter pair, but I’m afraid that size has sold out in black. We do have grey. ||Woman: [5]Grey would go with my jacket just as well, so I’ll take those instead[/5].

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Excuse me, I bought these trousers yesterday, but when I tried them on at home, they were far too long. I like the style, though.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: We can exchange them for a shorter pair, but I’m afraid that size has sold out in black. We do have grey.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Grey would go with my jacket just as well, so I’ll take those instead.

questions.question: What does the woman decide to do?

options.B: Exchange the trousers for a different colour.

questions.explanation: She agrees to take the shorter grey pair instead of the black trousers (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a teenager talking to his father about a school trip.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Dad, there’s a geography trip to the coast next month. It’s during the school week, so I won’t miss football on Saturday. ||Man: That sounds useful. Is it expensive? ||Boy: The coach journey and the museum visit are included. [6]The only thing we have to organise ourselves is food for the day, because there isn’t anywhere suitable to buy lunch[/6]. ||Man: We can prepare something the night before.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Dad, there’s a geography trip to the coast next month. It’s during the school week, so I won’t miss football on Saturday.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds useful. Is it expensive?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: The coach journey and the museum visit are included. The only thing we have to organise ourselves is food for the day, because there isn’t anywhere suitable to buy lunch.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: We can prepare something the night before.

questions.question: What will the boy need to take on the trip?
questions.explanation: He says students must organise their own food because there is nowhere suitable to buy lunch (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a meeting.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you ready for tomorrow’s meeting with the new manager? I’ve printed the sales figures and the customer survey. ||Man: Great. I’ve prepared the slides, but I’m not happy with the opening section. It goes on too long. ||Woman: [7]Send it to me this afternoon and I’ll see if I can make that first part shorter[/7]. The figures can stay as they are. ||Man: Thanks. That would really help.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you ready for tomorrow’s meeting with the new manager? I’ve printed the sales figures and the customer survey.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Great. I’ve prepared the slides, but I’m not happy with the opening section. It goes on too long.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Send it to me this afternoon and I’ll see if I can make that first part shorter. The figures can stay as they are.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Thanks. That would really help.

questions.question: What does the woman offer to do?
questions.explanation: She offers to shorten the opening section of his slides (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio presenter talking about a town food festival.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: The annual Riverside Food Festival returns on Sunday, with stalls selling dishes from around the world. Cooking demonstrations begin at midday, and entry to the park remains free. ||Presenter: Last year, many visitors had difficulty parking nearby. [8]This time, festival tickets include a free bus ride from the station, and organisers are encouraging everyone to use it[/8]. Details are available online.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: The annual Riverside Food Festival returns on Sunday, with stalls selling dishes from around the world. Cooking demonstrations begin at midday, and entry to the park remains free.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Last year, many visitors had difficulty parking nearby. This time, festival tickets include a free bus ride from the station, and organisers are encouraging everyone to use it. Details are available online.

questions.question: What is new about the food festival this year?

options.A: Visitors can attend free cooking lessons.
options.B: There will be more car-parking spaces.
options.C: Transport from the station is provided.

questions.explanation: The presenter says festival tickets include a free bus ride from the station this year (evidence [8]).
